entry.title: SAP Screen Personas and Adapt UI simplify classic apps. April 2025 roadmap update
entry.content:
Customer adoption of SAP Screen Personas and Adapt UI for classic apps within SAP S/4HANA Cloud continues to grow. The most popular scenarios in which people simplify the classic screens in public cloud environments are sales orders and purchase orders. In this post, I’ll discuss when to use SAP Screen Personas and SAP Fiori apps, the SAP Screen Personas roadmap, and some specific next steps for customers and partners to optimize your UX transformation journey.

SAP Screen Personas is one way to deliver the SAP Fiori user experience

In a recent call with a partner supporting a large retailer, they asked if their customer should use SAP Screen Personas to simplify the classic sales order apps or to use the latest SAP Fiori app for Manage Sales Orders. Generally, if there is an SAP Fiori app that SAP ships, we recommend you evaluate it. SAP Fiori apps based on SAP Fiori elements receive ongoing innovations, including embedded AI capabilities that continue to evolve.

Most organizations will use a combination of SAP-delivered Fiori apps, adapted SAP Fiori apps, SAP Screen Personas flavors for classic applications, and SAP Fiori elements apps that they build themselves to provide a complete and consistent UX to their users. So, you should probably use a combination of both SAP Screen Personas AND SAP Fiori.

SAP Screen Personas is one of several steps that SAP has taken over the past decade to make its ERP screen easier to use:

  • Creating 1000s of SAP Fiori apps to cover the most common business scenarios
  • Making SAP Screen Personas available to help customers simplify other transactions
  • Acquiring WalkMe to allow customers to annotate workflows in enterprise applications

Continuous improvement is part of the SAP UX Engineering culture

Within SAP Screen Personas, we are continuing to innovate to make it easier for you to streamline business processes that still rely on classic apps. In addition to all the basic screen editing functions that have been available for over a decade, we are making scripting more powerful and providing more capabilities to manage the tables that are so common in classic apps. With all the innovations in the SAP Fiori design system, we have also added more granular controls so you can give the classic screens the SAP Fiori user experience to improve usability and ensure consistency across the entire SAP portfolio.

SAP Screen Personas flavors can launch from My Home, alongside SAP Fiori apps, again providing a consistent way to access core ERP functionality. In short, the SAP Screen Personas roadmap focuses on continuing to innovate with new features and integrate into the rest of the portfolio so you can enjoy better usability and more UX consistency in your SAP ERP systems.

VA01, VA02, VA03, ME21N, ME22N, ME23N remain among the most common transactions

SAP Screen Personas is a key part of SAP’s user experience strategy

  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition – the ability to simplify classic screens is built into the Adapt UI menu. If you open it for an SAP Fiori app, you can edit the screen with SAPUI5 Flexibility. If you open Adapt UI in a classic app, you will launch the SAP Screen Personas screen editor.
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition – the add-on is available for download.
  • SAP S/4HANA (on-prem) and ECC – the process is the same as for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, since you control your IT environment.

Since we added it to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition as a standard feature, that should be solid evidence that the ability to adapt the UI of classic apps is something important to us. We are supporting SAP Screen Personas functionality through at least 2040.

SAP Screen Persons flavors are part of a clean core strategy, as they do not touch the core transactions / classic apps.

How to improve the user experience of your SAP ERP system

Customers

  • If you run SAP S/4HANA Cloud, SAP Fiori apps should be your go-to solution, whenever possible. The SAP Fiori app reference library has a list of all the apps that are available in your environment.
  • For classic applications that don’t have an SAP Fiori app, you can use the power of SAP Screen Personas to hide fields you don’t need, pre-fill content if the information does not change, or automate repetitive actions using the scripting engine.
  • SAP Screen Personas is built into SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition as part of the Adapt UI capabilities. For SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition and on-prem, you can install the add-on in less than an hour, if you know what you are doing. There is no additional license fee to use SAP Screen Personas in any environment.

Partners

SAP Screen Personas should be one of several key tools that are part of your UX transformation tool kit. If you don’t have expertise today, you can start with the free training course on SAP Screen Personas on the SAP Learning platform.

Learn more about SAP Screen Personas

  • Online training. The best way to learn the basics about SAP Screen Personas (Adapt UI for classic applications) is to take our course on the SAP Learning platform. https://learning.sap.com/courses/adapting-the-ui-in-sap-s-4hana-cloud-using-sap-screen-personas
  • Connect with your peers. Every other month, we have a group of customers that gather online to share best practices, demo their flavors, and answer each other’s questions. Experts from the product team also join to explain how to use the latest functionality. If you are interested in joining, you can sign up online to join the SAP Screen Personas practitioner forum. This will add you to the mailing list for the monthly meeting summaries. Please contact me directly if you want to join the monthly calls.

I hope you start using Adapt UI for Classic Apps or SAP Screen Personas to make your business users happier and more productive.

For the SAP UX Foundations team, Peter Spielvogel.
(Yes, this is a new name for our organization. Same mission, same goals. The new name reflects that we have product managers and UX experts working together with engineers to achieve our goal of delighting SAP customers with a better user experience in SAP S/4HANA.)

entry.title: Re: Welcome all SAP Community members, start here!
entry.content:
Hi, this is Samin and I’m currently completing the final semester of my MBA in International Business at Trinity Western University, Vancouver. I’m writing to express not only my interest in the HANA Technical Support internship, but also my deep and personal passion for SAP and a sincere request to explore any path to be part of your ecosystem.

My journey has not been linear, but it has been rich with purpose. I started my career after graduating in Computer Software Engineering, working with databases, SQL, and networks. Life’s circumstances later led me into instructing, but everything changed when I joined a large oil and gas project team in Iran. That opportunity brought me close to real-world engineering, procurement, contracts, and planning. I found myself engaging with business processes deeply: contributing to BPMS design, entering and validating contract data, supporting EDMS/PPMS system implementations (ERP), and even training users by translating complex systems into understandable workflows.

Through it all, I realized something: I was passionate about making processes smarter and more human, and SAP spoke that exact language. Since moving to Canada, I’ve joined the SAP community online, attended webinars, and continued learning on my own. I’ve been waiting for the right opportunity to align my lived experience with the structured, global impact that SAP creates.

I understand that I may not meet all the technical criteria listed to start very quick, but I am a fast learner with proven adaptability, cross-functional collaboration experience, and a strong desire to grow within the SAP ecosystem. I believe that with the right guidance and opportunity, I can grow quickly into a valuable contributor to SAP.

Even if I’m not the typical candidate, I would be incredibly grateful for any chance, even a short conversation, to learn more, share more, and explore how I might start from somewhere, however small, within your dynamic environment.

Thank you for considering my story and the potential behind it.

entry.title: Time Valution for Bank Holiday on Saturday
entry.content:
Dear Community members 

I would need your advice in one customer's requirement:

Let me exaplain you the internal rule for weekend/public holiday work with one simple example:

Employee which is supposed to work during the Saturday, Sunday and Bank holiday records to his time sheet a single working time type "Overtime". All hours recorded as "Overtime" are subject of following rules:

1. "Overtime" recorded on SUNDAY or PUBLIC HOLIDAY should be compensated with base hourly wage of a rate "A".

2. "Overtime" recorded on SATURDAY should be compensated with base hourly wage of a rate "B".

3. "Overtime" recorded on SATURDAY  and this Saturday is in the same time PUBLIC HOLIDAY, should be compensated ONLY with base hourly wage of a rate "A".

Do you have idea how to cover cases in example No.3 with some time valuations that ensures that employee is not compensated with both rates but the rate valid for Public holiday is chosen by the valuation?

 

Many thanks, Jana

SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central 

SAP SuccessFactors Time Tracking 

@Volker_Ruof

entry.title: A Step-By-Step Walkthrough for SAP Analytics Cloud compass (PART I)
entry.content:
With this two-part blog I would like to give an overview of the typical steps taken while simulating with compass to perform stochastic risk analysis (Monte Carlo Simulation), and also offer helpful hints along the way.

Blog 1:

#1 – Starting a simulation
#2 – Configuring drivers
#3 – Simulating

Blog 2:

#4 – Interpreting the Results
#5 – Save Simulation and Scenario Comparison
#6 – Collaboration and Sharing

Here we go!

#1- Starting a Simulation

A compass simulation can be started in two ways: Either directly via the dashboard by right clicking on a data cell, or via the compass start page, accessible via the toolbar on the left.

Via right-click on the data cell:

This is a perfect way for end users to begin simulation - directly from the dashboard. Note that the cell you clicked on will not be the driver but the target of the simulation, meaning the impact which you want to explore. Hence when starting from a dashboard, think in terms of “Which impact should be explored”, instead of “which drivers should be used”.
Starting from the dashboard will create a simulation scenario directly for you, with auto-detection of all filter context from the dashboard and all available drivers for the chosen target. We aim that from 2025 Q2 onwards, a compass simulation can be opened in a new tab, allowing easy navigation between simulation and the dashboard.
If you are not seeing the option "Create Compass Simulation" upon right-click on a data cell, check with your IT admin the following:
- Whether you have been assigned the rights to create simulation in your role permission setup
- Whether the dashboard has been created with "optimised design experience" (Compass does not support classic stories.)

Via compass start page:

This is more suitable for technical / power users who know the data model and the relationship between the KPIs well enough to configure the simulation target and filters without the help of a dashboard. IT users seeking to create a simulation with preferred drivers and/or ranges already set up for the end users (e.g. to ease their simulation workflow) can also consider starting directly here. It is not compulsory to first find a dashboard with the desired context before a simulation can be started.
After the configuration of the simulation settings, the creation of the first scenario will follow and needs to be completed with details to name, description and the version of data for baseline.

After the creation of the first scenario, there will be no difference in workflow between simulation created via compass start page and a dashboard. Note that after the creation of a simulation, it is not possible to change the setup for the simulation settings without deleting all existing scenarios. This is firstly to ensure that all created scenarios share the same simulation target settings to remain comparable. Another reason being that changes in the simulation settings will alter the required simulation calculation and driver, thus leading to the irrelevancy of previously created scenarios. For the future we are looking into enhancing the experience here for more flexibility.   

Set up scenarios:

Before we move on, it is important to understand the difference between a “simulation” and a “scenario”. A simulation is the main compass artefact, which contains the configuration of the chosen target KPI. A simulation itself can contain one or multiple scenarios, each modelling different assumptions of driver uncertainties and delivering different results regarding the impact on the target KPI.

While simulation settings cannot be altered after simulation creation, scenario settings can be adjusted anytime. Besides name, description and the version of data for baseline, an important feature here is the ability to setup additional filters for the drivers. Note that the filters added to a scenario will apply to all drivers during the calculation. This is to ease the mass adjustment. For more detailed adjustments, see the section below.

#2 - Configuring Drivers

Automatic detection

The first step of your simulation would be to model your assumptions by configuring the drivers. As mentioned above, compass automatically detects the relevant drivers for your chosen target – these are the leaf account members and / or base measures that contribute to the calculation of the target. For the detection to be possible, the relationship between the target and the drivers will need to be already defined in the SAP Analytics Cloud model via by at least one of the following:

  • Account dimension hierarchy
  • Account formula
  • Calculated measure

Note that the account dimension is not mandatory for a compass simulation. If the model contains only calculated measures and /or base measure, simulation is still possible. If simulating with a calculated measure as target, the detected driver will be the base measures contributing to the calculation, whereas if simulating with a base measure (without formula) as target, the detected driver will be the base measure itself. In the latter case, it might seem confusing at first glance to have the same measure as driver and target, but this is actually useful for simulating the accumulation of uncertainties for the same measure. This could be done by adding restricted drivers to simulate the impact on an aggregated value. For example, if simulating the impact on “Sales Volume”, which is a base measure without formula, the driver detected will be “Sales Volume”, and restricted drivers should be manually created so that e.g. uncertainties in e.g. China and Thailand can be entered as value ranges to simulate the impact on total sales volume in Asia. (Learn more about restricted drivers below)

Trimming the driver list

Even though compass displayed all relevant drivers for your chosen target, it is not necessary to simulate uncertainties for all them. You can choose to focus on just a few and remove the rest from the driver list by clicking on the “X” for each driver. Note that you are not removing those drivers from the simulation calculation, but just removing them from view. Their currently baseline (i.e. the currently booked value in the model) will still be used for the calculation of the target; there will just be no range of uncertainty to be taken into consideration for those removed drivers.

To illustrate this with an easy example: Let’s say you would like to simulate the impact on “Revenue”, using the drivers “Price” and “Volume” with “Revenue = Price*Volume”.  Now even if you remove the driver “Price” from the list, you are not changing the calculation to “Revenue = Volume”, but instead, you are just focusing on simulating the uncertainty in “Volume”, by entering a range within which the “Volume” could fluctuate.

The same logic applies for all of the following:

  1. Removing a driver from the “Select Driver” dialog
  2. Removing a driver via clicking “X”
  3. Deactivating a driver via the toggle
  4. Leaving the value configuration for a driver blank

In all of these situations the baseline is used for the calculation and no simulation of uncertainty via random sampling for the drivers is performed. (For more on random sampling, please refer to the blog for introduction to Monte Carlo Simulation.) The different ways of “removing” the uncertainty effect from a driver are meant to serve different workflows, which could be the trimming of driver list down to only drivers of interest ( via “Select Driver” dialog or  “X”), or the temporary deactivating/activating of uncertainty effect without having to remodel the driver ranges to re-test again.

Value configuration / distribution type

After having reduced the driver list down to those of your interest, the next step would be to model the uncertainties by entering the min and max values for each driver required for the simulation.

Note here that the baseline shown next to the min and max input are only serving as visual guidance; it does not pose any limit to the min or max input, but merely gives an idea of how far your simulated uncertainties are from the currently booked value in the model.

When performing a scenario calculation, the system randomly samples a value within the range you have configured, taking the distribution type for each driver into consideration. By default, all drivers will perform the random sampling using the normal distribution type, which means simply that the values in the middle will have a higher chance of being sampled. If desired, you can configure the distribution type to be uniform and assign equal likelihood of being sampled to all values within the range. Do not worry if you are not seeing the distribution type on the driver list – it is hidden per default, since in most use cases there is no need to alter the default setting of normal distribution. To change this is easy; simply click on the settings icon on the top right of the driver list and select the column “distribution”.


Click on the settings icon to open the driver list settings.


Check "Distribution" to bring the column into the drivers list

Once the column is visible, click on the dropdown to change the distribution type.

(Learn more about distribution types under “Distribution” in the help portal).

Restricted Drivers

Now, if the intention is not to perform a high-level but rather a more precise simulation that takes geographical, temporal, or other nuances of uncertainties into consideration, you can use restricted drivers to achieve this. For example, if simulating sales volume, instead of entering a global uncertainty of 3500 to 4500, it is possible to split the min and max effects, for example, by simulating a sales volume for Germany which is between 2000 to 3000 and for India which is between 1500 to 2500. You can do this by creating a restricted driver for each of the respective region and entering the corresponding min and max value. In such manner, the geographical impact for other volume-dependent KPIs  (e.g. revenue, expenses) can also be taken into consideration. Restricted drivers can also be created for more than one dimension, enabling thus a multidimensional simulation.

Creating a restricted driver is easy; simply click on the “+” displayed next to the activate/deactivate toggle.

You will be prompted to select the exact filter context for the driver. It could be a multidimensional filter. Note here that when you select e.g. two regions, a single driver restricted down to both regions will be created, and not a separate driver for each region.

#3 – Simulating

Three running modes

Once you are done configuring the drivers to model your assumptions of the driver uncertainties, you can run the scenario to observe the calculated impact. There are three calculation modes to choose from:

  • Preview (1000 calculations*)
  • Medium Precision (10 000 calculations*)
  • High Precision (100 000 repeated calculations*)

*calculations = repeated calculations for each scenario run

Note that the higher the number of calculations, the longer the scenario run will take, and the more simulation results will be generated, leading to higher precision. (For those unfamiliar with the concept of repeated calculation in Monte Carlo Simulations, there is an introduction blog with an overview. )
The Preview mode, with 1000 calculations, will give you the fastest simulation, but the lowest precision as the results generated are based on the lowest number of repeated calculations from all three modes. This means that if running two separate preview runs, the results obtained both will most likely have a bigger variance than when performing two Medium or High Precision runs.  The right choice of calculation mode is hence dependent on the intention. If the focus is on attaining a quick estimation to e.g. derive important drivers, using the preview mode would be sufficient. If the focus is on understanding the possible risk distribution of impact, it would be recommended to perform a Medium or High Precision run.

---
This part I of the two-blog series will have given you hopefully a good understanding on how to start simulating. Continue reading part II for result interpretation, scenario comparison and collaboration with compass!

entry.title: A Step-By-Step Walkthrough for SAP Analytics Cloud compass (PART II)
entry.content:
This is part II of the two-blog series meant to give an overview of the typical steps taken while simulating with compass. This blog picks up from the part directly after having triggered a scenario run. Just as a recap, below you can see the structure of the two blogs:

Blog 1:

#1 – Starting a simulation
#2 – Configuring drivers
#3 – Simulating

Blog 2:

#4 – Interpreting the Results
#5 – Save Simulation and Scenario Comparison
#6 – Collaboration and Sharing

Let's dive in!

#4 - Interpreting the Results

Result components

Once the simulation calculations are completed, the probability distribution of the impact will be displayed in a graph, which is divided into 3 segments:  the optimistic, pessimistic and realistic case. By default, they are defined as such: Pessimistic (lowest 5% of the results), optimistic (highest 5% of the results) and realistic (90% of the results in-between). The definition of the percentages can be changed via the “case settings” configurations, but 5/90/5 percentage division is among the most common. The corresponding result ranges for each case is visible on the graph and also in the three boxes reserved for the cases below. You will also see a slider which is parked at the current baseline, which is the current value booked in the model for the target KPI, marked also on the X-Axis with “Baseline (current value)”

Understanding the results

Interpreting the simulation results require a look at all three main components together: (1) The graph, (2) the position of the current baseline including the percentages displayed, (3) and the results ranges in the boxes below.

In the example above, you can see that the current baseline is lying in the realistic area of the graph. This indicates that the current booked value in the model is within the attained results but not within the edge cases (the 5% highest or lowest value) and hence has a realistic chance of being achieved from a statistical point of view. The 16% on the right of the slider means that there is a 16% chance of achieving a value higher than the current baseline of 215.81 mUSD. But note that since the optimistic case (in the rightmost box at the bottom) only stretches to 246.91 mUSD, this means that there is a 16% chance to achieve a value between 215.81 to 246.91 mUSD. Similarly, for the 84% on the left side of the slider, it means that there is a 84% of achieving a value between 215.81 to 158.21 mUSD, which is the lowest value attainable in the pessimistic case.

Now some of you might be wondering why there is a slider in the graph. This slider actually allows the exploration of chances associated with a specific value. Let’s say if you are interested in what are the chances of achieving 200 mUSD under the conditions you have modelled in the scenario, simply drag the slider to the position of 200 mUSD or enter “200” in the input box. The percentages display on the left and right side will be recalculated and are to be interpreted as explained in the paragraph above.

#5 – Save Simulation and Scenario Comparison

The significance of saving a simulation

Once done with the simulation, you could proceed to save it in the file repository by clicking on the save icon next to the simulation or scenario name. Saving a simulation has two advantages: (1) It is possible to return at a later point in time and compare the results to the actual performance, enabling insight into the accuracy of the assumptions made and the results observed. If performed over time, this approach will promote understanding of the important drivers and how to simulate more accurately. (2) Saving a simulation allows the creation of multiple scenarios*, which can be compared against each other to understand the results obtainable via different assumptions.  
(* Working with multiple scenarios require the saving of changes in each scenario before moving on to work in another. This is to reduce the ambiguity regarding the actuality of the results when comparing the scenarios.)

Comparing scenarios

In the above example, you can see that it is possible to select another scenario via the dropdown in the header. The result of the selected scenario will appear at the bottom in the three boxes designated for the results of the pessimistic, realistic and optimistic cases. This is to enable the comparison of the results across different scenarios at a glance, giving immediate insight to how each scenario performs in each case.  

Helpful Hint

It is recommendable to simulate a first scenario with value ranges reflecting the uncertainties in real life to understand how hard the fluctuations would hit the business and where the company would stand in worst and best cases. After gaining insight here, it would make sense to create further scenarios with value ranges reflecting the assumptions of how the company could mitigate the risk with counter strategies. The scenarios could be then compared to assist strategic decision. Note that during scenario comparison, a scenario giving a worse result in worst case might deliver a better result in optimistic case, hence it is always recommendable to look at both ends of the result when assessing the impact.
Remember also to give each scenario a good description that describes the intention behind the value ranges given. As all scenarios could possibly share the same drivers, it will become difficult after some time to recall the intended strategy behind the value input for each scenario just by observing the numbers. If possible, try to start with a brief description first such as “investing in marketing in Asia”, and elaborate it as you move on. The description is going to be your best friend here; it is actually one of the most useful features, together with the graph and value ranges, to give clue to the strategy modelled.

It is also useful to keep in mind the following: Depending on the answers you are seeking using compass, the answer might be contained at times within the result graph, and at times within the value configuration in the driver list.
For example: In the first scenario with value ranges reflecting the uncertainties in real life, the answer you are seeking here is the possible risk distribution of impact, which is to be found within the result graph. However, when modelling counter strategy with further scenarios, the result graph of each scenario becomes more of a reference for the possible effectiveness of your assumptions. Here the answer sought is hence actually in the value configuration of the specific scenario with the most appealing result for you, giving insight to the ranges which should be applied for the chosen drivers to attain the acceptable risk context.

#6 – Collaboration and Sharing

Collection of assumptions across departments

When you are done with simulation, you might want to share your results with fellow colleagues or a decision maker. Or you might want to invite responsible colleagues to simulate together with you. With compass, it is possible to simulate with multiple colleagues together, or even across departments. The collaboration aspect is crucial, because more often than not, the knowledge of the driver uncertainties (i.e. the min and max value per driver) across different regions and products are not centrally located but is dispersed across different (global) departments. The collaboration feature here will enable a structured environment for collective enterprise simulation.

Private and Public scenarios

Maybe you have noticed by now that when you start your simulation, the scenario created is always a private scenario, meaning that it is only visible to you and no one else. This ensures discretion during the simulation process. Once you have decided on a scenario which you would like to show colleagues, you can do so by publishing a scenario AND sharing the saved simulation artefact with the intended colleague(s), just like when you would share a story or a model with them. Note that publishing a scenario is done within the compass UI (in the scenario header), and sharing is performed in the file repository. If the publishing icon is deactivated for you, it means that there are unsaved changes in your scenario. Simply click the save icon, then publish afterwards.

Colleagues, with whom you have shared the simulation, will only see the public scenarios, which is in a read-only state. If desired, they can create a private copy of the public scenario, add their value configuration, run the scenario calculation and then publish it again, either overwriting the current public scenario or publishing as a new one. Note that while it is possible to explore the public scenario results by e.g. moving the slider, these changes will not be saved.

---

This is all for now and I hope the step-by-step guidance could give you more insights and useful hints on how compass could be utilised to not only help you understand the risk at hand, but also how to mitigate it.

We are working constantly to improve compass! Do leave us a note in the influence portal to let us know what you feel could be the next enhancements. We monitor the portal closely for your voices!

entry.title: Performance Issues when loading and exporting data from a custom table via SE16N
entry.content:
We are unable to load and export the same amount of data from a custom table in SE16N as we are in the non-upgraded environments. The system will time out with an ABAP runtime error. Further details are in the attachment.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Performance Issues when loading and exporting data fr...
entry.content:
As you may know that SE16 / SE16N is opening coding tool and not officially release to customer, there is no support for such open coding tool.

- reference

3251583 - Performance issues when viewing database views from SE16 or SE16N
=>
SE16 and SE16N are generic data browsers that cannot guarantee high performance in any case (see SAP Note 1602869).

1602869 - Bad performance when using transaction SE16 or SE16N to browse data
=>
SE16 and SE16N are not intended to serve as tool for executing special queries as due to their generic nature they cannot guarantee good performance for specific queries. This behavior is not a malfunction or glitch but it works as designed.

You could try to follow the note from your side and test these hints to see if you are the case.

- hint
LIMIT_THRU_JOIN
PRELIMIT_BEFORE_JOIN
NO_JOIN_THRU_AGGR

entry.title: SAP Inside Track Tokyo 2025 Event Report
entry.content:
SAP Inside Track Tokyo 2025 (sitTokyo2025)は、SAP技術の最新状況や自身の活動・経験を紹介するエキサイティングなイベントでした。3日間にわたって開催され、多様なSAP愛好者や専門家が集まりました。
SAP Inside Track Tokyo 2025 (sitTokyo2025) was an exciting event that showcased the latest advancements in SAP technology and provided a platform for participants to share their activities and experiences. The event was held over three days and attracted a diverse group of SAP enthusiasts and professionals.


イベント概要/Event Summary

このイベントは3日間に分けて行われ、それぞれ異なるSAPテーマとその応用に焦点を当てました。sitTokyo2025は、Connect Together, Better Together ～ ともに繋がり、ともに良くなる ～をコミュニティテーマに掲げ、今年も多くの有志によって開催をすることができました。イベント開催をサポートしてくれた運営メンバーの皆様、登壇してくれた皆様、本当にありがとうございました！ 今年のイベントでは、ユーザー企業からの登壇者が出てくれたこと、そしてJSUG (Japan SAP User Group)とのコラボ実現、新しいコミュニティメンバーの登壇と、これまでのコミュニティ活動の成果と今後の広がりを期待できる変化を感られました。これは大きな収穫でした。

The event was divided into three days, each focusing on different SAP themes and their applications.

sitTokyo2025 was held under the community theme "Connect Together, Better Together",  thanks to the efforts of many volunteers. We would like to extend our heartfelt gratitude to the organizing members who supported the event and the presenters who took the stage!  

This year's event featured speakers from user companies, collaboration with JSUG (Japan SAP User Group), and presentations from new community members, reflecting the achievements of past community activities and the promising expansion in the future.

３日間のイベントのスタッツは以下の通りです。
Here are the stats for the three-day event.

Attendee TTL: 307 / Session TTL: 16 / Event Satisfaction: 4.89 / 5.0pt

Date Track Topics
2/28 (Fri)Digital Transformation with Cloud ERP (DT) & Artificial Intelligence (AI)Attendees: 101
3/14 (Fri)Application Development and Automation (ADA) & Integration (IN)Attendees: 122
3/19 (Wed)Data and Analytics (DA)Attendees: 84

 

Sumikko さんが投稿したSAP Inside Track Tokyo 2025 Event Informationにも詳細が記載されていますので、より詳しい情報や動画をみたい方はそちらをご確認ください。

For detailed information about the event, please refer to the SAP Inside Track Tokyo 2025 Event Information posted by Sumikko. You can find information about the speakers and more there[^i^]. 

 

以下は各日の主なハイライトについてまとめておきます。
Below are the main highlights of each day.

Day 1

28th-Feb. Digital Transformation with Cloud ERP (DT)&Artificial Intelligence (AI)

セッションでは、SAUG-x / TechEd on tour Melbourneでの経験と学び、BTPデリバリーパートナー支援プログラム、RISE with SAPプロジェクトの悩み共有、JSUGテクニカル部会とASUG Tech Connectの紹介、Signavio Process Navigatorを使用したCloud ERPプロジェクトの推進法、ABAP開発における生成AI機能についての発表が行われました[^i^]。
The sessions included experiences and learnings from SAUG-x / TechEd on tour Melbourne, the BTP Delivery Partner Support Program, sharing challenges in RISE with SAP projects, an introduction to the JSUG Technical Committee and ASUG Tech Connect, promoting Cloud ERP projects using Signavio Process Navigator, and presentations on generative AI functionalities in ABAP development[^i^].

Day1 event youtube：http://youtu.be/1F2R8MJKv0Y

やはりAI関連のトピックスは関心が高くほぼ全てのセッションにAIに関するテーマがありましたね。内容はイベントに参加して得たSAPのAIに関する学びを帰ってから実際に試してみた話や、Jouleを日本のマーケットで利用できるようになるための情報収集から得た学びの共有、ABAP開発者の視点からのJouleへの期待、SAPのサポート部門でのAI活用事例など、多岐にわたる内容でした。それ以外にも若手の方がRISEプロジェクトで経験した学びについて共有いただいたり、Signavio Process NavigatorなどSAPが提供するプロジェクトツールの有効な活用方法などを共有いただきました。

Indeed, AI-related topics garnered significant interest, with almost all sessions featuring themes related to AI. The content ranged from sharing experiences of trying out SAP's AI learnings gained from the event, to discussing insights gathered for making Joule available in the Japanese market, expectations for Joule from the perspective of ABAP developers, and examples of AI utilization in SAP's support department. Additionally, young professionals shared their learnings from RISE projects, and effective utilization methods of project tools provided by SAP, such as Signavio Process Navigator[^i^].

登壇資料サマリー一例 /「Here are partial excerpts from session」

 


Day 2

14th-Mar.  Application Development and Automation (AD) & Integration (IN)

SAP Inside Track Tokyo 2025のDay2には、Clean CoreとBTPの両立実践、SAP Event Meshで始めるイベント・ドリブン・アーキテクチャ、S/4HANA・BTP開発技術者育成の取り組み、開発者拡張とIntegration Suiteを使ったインタフェース機能の開発、SAP BPAの最新機能の紹介、生成AI活用のためのPythonアプリ開発@Cloud Foundry runtimeなどのセッションが提供されました[^i^]。
On Day 2 of SAP Inside Track Tokyo 2025, sessions were provided on topics such as the practical implementation of Clean Core and BTP, starting event-driven architecture with SAP Event Mesh, initiatives for developing S/4HANA and BTP development engineers, developing interface functions using developer extensions and Integration Suite, introducing the latest features of SAP BPA, and developing Python applications utilizing generative AI on SAP BTP Cloud Foundry runtime[^i^].


Day2 Event youtube：https://youtu.be/ywP3LjdQ0FA

S/4HANA Cloudを導入し、Clean Coreを実践するために、どのような導入を意識するのか？システムの現状を可視化し最適な方法を選択するためのツールや手法を理解し、提案・デリバリできる人材を育成、BTPを利用した機能拡張・インテグレーションやビジネスAI活用のTipまで、統合的に理解できるものになっています。

To implement S/4HANA Cloud and practice Clean Core, it is essential to consider the approach to implementation. This involves visualizing the current state of the system, understanding tools and methods to select the optimal approach, and developing personnel who can propose and deliver solutions. The session covers tips for utilizing BTP for functional extensions, integrations, and business AI, providing a comprehensive understanding[^i^].

登壇資料サマリー一例 /「Here are partial excerpts from session」

 


Day 3

19th-Mar. Data and Analytics (DA)

SAP Inside Track Tokyo 2025のDay3では、AIを活用したSAP Analytics Cloudの「Just Ask」機能を使ったデータモデルの可視化、SAC/Datasphereの最新アップデート情報、SAP Signavio Process Insightsを使ったS/4HANAとERP 6.0の接続事例、そしてSAP TechEd 2024（メルボルン）でのData and Analyticsに関する学びが紹介されました。特に、実際のデモや具体的な手順を含むセッションが多く、参加者にとって非常に実践的で有益な内容となりました[^i^]。

On Day 3 of SAP Inside Track Tokyo 2025, sessions covered various topics including utilizing AI in SAP Analytics Cloud with the "Just Ask" feature for visualizing data models built on Datasphere, the latest updates on SAC/Datasphere focusing on planning functionalities, connecting SAP Signavio Process Insights with S/4HANA and ERP 6.0, and learnings from SAP TechEd 2024 in Melbourne centered around Data and Analytics. The sessions were highly practical and beneficial, featuring actual demos and detailed procedures[^i^].

Day3 Event youtube：https://youtu.be/lKFXUAeIGz4

コミュニティのベテランから若手、そして最も注目すべきはユーザー企業からも登壇者が出たことです。そしてその発表の内容も実際のプロジェクトに基づく素晴らしいものでとても価値の高いものでした。そしてここでもAIは重要なテーマとして取り扱われていました。

From seasoned community members to young professionals, and most notably, speakers from user companies also took the stage. Their presentations were based on actual projects and were highly valuable. AI was also treated as an important theme throughout[^i^].

登壇資料サマリー一例 /「Here are partial excerpts from session」

 

 

参加者のフィードバック / Feed Back from Audience

  • 全体的な評価 / Overall Evaluation:

    • 参加者はイベントを非常に楽しんだ様子で、「楽しかった」「最高でした」「勉強になりました」といったコメントが多く寄せられました。
    • 初めて参加した人々も「初めて参加しましたが、とても良かったです」「初参加でした、楽しかったです！」といったポジティブな感想をいただけました。
    • Participants seemed to thoroughly enjoy the event, with many comments such as "It was fun," "It was great," and "I learned a lot."
    • First-time attendees also shared positive feedback like "It was my first time attending, and it was very good," and "It was my first time, and I had fun!"

  • 学びと刺激 / Learning and Inspiration:

    • 多くの参加者がイベントを通じて新しい知識を得たことを強調しています。「勉強になりました」「新しい技術を扱えるプロジェクトに入りたい」「最新の技術キャッチアップできました」といったコメントが多く寄せられていました。
    • また、「良い刺激になりました」「技術トークはワクワクしますね！」といった感想もあり、イベントが参加者にとって刺激的であったことが伺えますね。
    • Many participants emphasized that they gained new knowledge through the event, with comments like "I learned a lot," "I want to join a project that handles new technology," and "I was able to catch up on the latest technology."
    • Additionally, there were remarks such as "It was a good stimulus," and "Tech talks are exciting!" indicating that the event was stimulating for the participants.

  • 運営と登壇者への感謝 /  Appreciation for Organizers and Speakers : 

    • イベントの運営に対する感謝の言葉も多く、また、登壇者に対しても「登壇者お疲れ様でした」「登壇緊張しましたが、楽しかったです！」といった感謝の言葉が多く寄せられています。運営として嬉しい限りです。
    • There were many words of gratitude towards the event organizers, as well as the speakers, with comments like "Thank you for the smooth operation," "Thank you to all the organizers," "Good job to the speakers," and "I was nervous about speaking, but it was fun!"

  • 次回への期待 / Expectations for the Next Event:

    • 多くの参加者が次回のイベントへの期待を示しており、「次は現地参加です！」「次は後輩連れて現地参加したいなぁ」といったコメントがありました、こんな人と人のつながりがコミュニティを強く大きくしていくんだなーと嬉しく思います。
    • また、「次の登壇ネタ作るぞ」「次は僕だ」といった意欲的なコメントもあり、参加者が次回のイベントに向けて積極的に準備を進めている様子が伺えますね。ぜひお待ちしてます！！
    • Many participants expressed their expectations for the next event, with comments like "Next time, I'll attend in person!" and "Next time, I want to bring my juniors and attend in person."
    • There were also enthusiastic comments like "I'll prepare the next presentation topic," and "Next time, it's me," showing that participants are actively preparing for the next event.

 

最後に / Wrap up

SAP Inside Track Tokyo 2025は、SAPの専門家や愛好者が知識を共有し、ネットワーキングし、SAP技術の未来を探求するための素晴らしいイベントでした。魅力的なセッションと洞察に満ちたプレゼンテーションは、SAPに興味のあるすべての人にとって必見のイベントでした。

SAP Inside Track Tokyo 2025 was a fantastic event where SAP experts and enthusiasts shared knowledge, networked, and explored the future of SAP technology. The engaging sessions and insightful presentations made it a must-attend event for anyone interested in SAP.

イベントの詳細な内容は、Sumikko さんが投稿したSAP Inside Track Tokyo 2025 Event Informationにもありますので、登壇者の情報などはそちらを参考にしてください。

For detailed information about the event, please refer to the SAP Inside Track Tokyo 2025 Event Information posted by Sumikko. You can find information about the speakers and more there.

このイベントのサマリーをお楽しみいただけたでしょうか。YouTubeリンクをチェックして、セッションをフルでご覧ください。次回のSAP Inside Trackイベントでお会いできることを楽しみにしています！

I hope you enjoyed this summary of the event. Check out the YouTube links to watch the sessions in full. We look forward to seeing you at the next SAP Inside Track event![^i^]

運営メンバーの皆さん、本当にありがとうございました。皆さんの一人一人の努力・支援に支えられ今年も素晴らしいイベントになったと思います。この場をお借りして改めて感謝したいと思います。

Thank you very much to all the members of the organizing team. I believe this year's event was wonderful, thanks to the efforts and support of each and every one of you. I would like to take this opportunity to express my gratitude once again.

As such, I am seeking clarification here.

Thanks a lot for any pointers on this.

Regards

SFC

entry.title: Re: Process Order Not creating PMR in EWM 950
entry.content:
Hi David,

First of all in your scenario, you do not need PMR ( Production MAterial Request),only you need goods Receipt in EWM.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_EXTENDED_WAREHOUSE_MANAGEMENT/3d97bec9bf1649099384bb8167df3cf2/1b7fec5...

Please note- EGR process is not link to PMR directly, you can run EGR Process explicitly without PMR.

Meaning of PMR:- Please understand this-PMR use only for  Staging and Consumption ( For your case, this is not EWM managed). You use the production material request for planning and monitoring the supply of products to production, and for the consumption of products by production, using advanced production integration.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_EXTENDED_WAREHOUSE_MANAGEMENT/3d97bec9bf1649099384bb8167df3cf2/c399eb535cbb5d1ee10000000a441470.html

 

"I need this Process Order to feed EWM so I have a PMR generated which I can then create Expected Goods Receipts against." -> 

EGR Generation:

steps to create Master Data/ Configuration:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_EXTENDED_WAREHOUSE_MANAGEMENT/3d97bec9bf1649099384bb8167df3cf2/c9e9a85296007b6ae10000000a423f68.html

Please let us know if further questions.

Kind Regards,

Ajit

entry.title: Re: Imputed interest for assets in SAP Public Cloud
entry.content:
Dear FabianSCC,

The depreciation key provided by SAP standard cannot be modified. If you need to create a custom depreciation key, please open a case for the XX-S4C-SRV-CON component. The experts for this component will help you create a new depreciation key that suits your requirements.

Have a nice day.

Best Regards,

Evelyn

entry.title: Update/Modify any Table data dynamically
entry.content:
To update any table dynamically in an SAP ABAP program, the general approach involves working with dynamic internal tables. This can be useful when you don't know the table's structure at compile-time, and you need to determine the table structure at runtime.

In this scenario, I'll provide a more detailed breakdown, including how you can dynamically:

  1.     Define a dynamic table at runtime.
  2.     Insert data into it.
  3.     Update rows based on a condition.
  4.     Handle different types of data and tables.

This example will be more general and applicable to any table in SAP.

Detailed Steps for Dynamic Table Updates

1. Create a Dynamic Table

In ABAP, you use CREATE DATA to create a dynamic internal table. You also need to dynamically assign a structure for the table using the REF TO DATA concept.

2. Field Symbol for Dynamic Data

A field symbol is used to refer to the data in a dynamic table. This allows you to access and modify the contents of the table.

3. Inserting Data into the Dynamic Table

You can append data into the dynamic table by dynamically creating entries.

4. Updating Data in the Dynamic Table

To update data, you 'will loop through the table and check conditions to identify which rows need modification. You will then use the MODIFY statement to update the content.

Example Code: Dynamic Table Creation, Insertion, and Update

Handling Other SAP Tables Dynamically

The above code works with the SFFLIGHT table. However, you can adopt this to any table in SAP by adjusting the structure and fields of the dynamic table.

To generalize for any SAP table, you'd need to know the structure of the table you're dealing with. You can retrieve the table's structure dynamically using function modules or predefined structures for commonly used SAP tables.

Example: Dynamically Handling Different Tables

If you want to dynamically choose which SAP table to work with, here’s a more flexible approach: &---------------------------------------------------------------------*

*& Report ZUT_UPLOAD_EXCEL_TO_DB_TABLE

*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*

*&

*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*

REPORT zut_upload_excel_to_db_table.

*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*

*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*

*& Global Data Definitions

*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*

DATA gt_data TYPE REF TO data.

DATA gs_data TYPE REF TO data.

 

FIELD-SYMBOLS: <ft_data> TYPE STANDARD TABLE,

               <fs_data> TYPE any.

 

"Table Structure

DATA: gt_dd03p TYPE TABLE OF dd03p.

FIELD-SYMBOLS: <fs_dd03p> TYPE dd03p.

 

"Error Report

DATA: gs_error_report TYPE bapiret2,

      gt_error_report TYPE TABLE OF bapiret2.

 

*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*

*& Selection Screen Definition

*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*

SELECTION-SCREEN BEGIN OF BLOCK b01 WITH FRAME TITLE TEXT-b01.

  PARAMETERS: p_table TYPE dd02l-tabname OBLIGATORY.

  SELECTION-SCREEN SKIP.

  PARAMETERS: p_file LIKE rlgrap-filename OBLIGATORY MEMORY ID zmdmfile.

  SELECTION-SCREEN SKIP.

  SELECTION-SCREEN BEGIN OF LINE.

    SELECTION-SCREEN COMMENT 1(30) TEXT-rfr.

    PARAMETERS: p_refr  RADIOBUTTON GROUP r01.

  SELECTION-SCREEN END OF LINE.

  SELECTION-SCREEN BEGIN OF LINE.

    SELECTION-SCREEN COMMENT 1(30) TEXT-ups.

    PARAMETERS: p_upsrt RADIOBUTTON GROUP r01.

  SELECTION-SCREEN END OF LINE.

SELECTION-SCREEN END OF BLOCK b01.

 

*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*

*& PAI/PBO Processing

*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*

AT SELECTION-SCREEN ON VALUE-REQUEST FOR p_file.

 

  "F4 for File name

  PERFORM f4_file_name USING 'P_FILE' CHANGING p_file.

 

AT SELECTION-SCREEN ON p_table.

 

  "Validate the Table/Input

  PERFORM validate_table .

 

*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*

*& Start-of-Selection Event

*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*

START-OF-SELECTION.

 

  "Initialization

  PERFORM initialization.

 

  "Get Excel data in internal table

  PERFORM convert_excel.

 

  "Upload data to database table

  PERFORM upload_table.

 

*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*

*& End-of-Selection Event

*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*

END-OF-SELECTION.

 

  "Display ALV Report

  PERFORM display_report.

 

*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*

*& Form f4_file_name

*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*

*& text

*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*

*& -->  p1        text

*& <--  p2        text

*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*

FORM f4_file_name USING p_field TYPE any

                  CHANGING p_file TYPE any.

 

  "F4 on File Name

  CALL FUNCTION 'KD_GET_FILENAME_ON_F4'

    EXPORTING

      program_name  = syst-repid

      dynpro_number = syst-dynnr

      field_name    = p_field

      static        = ' '

      mask          = '*.XLSX'

      fileoperation = 'R'

      path          = 'C:\TEMP\'

    CHANGING

      file_name     = p_file

    EXCEPTIONS

      mask_too_long = 1

      OTHERS        = 2.

  IF sy-subrc <> 0.

    MESSAGE ID sy-msgid TYPE sy-msgty NUMBER sy-msgno

    WITH sy-msgv1 sy-msgv2 sy-msgv3 sy-msgv4.

  ENDIF.

 

ENDFORM.

*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*

*& Form validate_table

*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*

*& text

*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*

*& -->  p1        text

*& <--  p2        text

*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*

FORM validate_table .

 

  "Local Variables

  DATA: ls_dd02l TYPE dd02l,

        ls_tvarvc type tvarvc.  " C2-8270 C2-8270

  CONSTANTS : lc_mhk_table(15) type c VALUE 'Z_MHK_TABLE' ." added-C2-8270

 

  "Upload only Tables in customer namespace

  IF p_table+0(1) NE 'Z' AND p_table+0(1) NE 'Y'.

    MESSAGE 'Only Y or Z tables can be uploaded' TYPE 'E'.

    RETURN.

  ENDIF.

 

*— Only transparent tables can be uploaded

  SELECT SINGLE *

   FROM dd02l

   INTO ls_dd02l

  WHERE tabname = p_table.

  IF sy-subrc EQ 0.

    IF ls_dd02l-tabclass NE 'TRANSP'.

      MESSAGE 'Only transparent TABLES can be uploaded' TYPE 'E'.

    ENDIF.

  ELSE.

    MESSAGE 'Invalid Table Name' TYPE 'E'.

    RETURN.

  ENDIF.

*BOC - C2-8270

    SELECT SINGLE *

  INTO ls_tvarvc

  FROM tvarvc

  WHERE name = lc_mhk_table

  and  low = P_table.

 

   if sy-subrc NE 0.

      MESSAGE 'Only TVARVC entry Table can be uploaded TYPE ' TYPE 'E'.

    ENDIF.

*EOC - C2-8270

ENDFORM.

*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*

*& Form convert_excel

*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*

*& text

*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*

*& -->  p1        text

*& <--  p2        text

*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*

FORM convert_excel .

 

  "Local Variables

  DATA lt_raw_data  TYPE truxs_t_text_data.

 

  "Excel Functions

  CALL FUNCTION 'TEXT_CONVERT_XLS_TO_SAP'

    EXPORTING

*     I_FIELD_SEPERATOR    =

      i_line_header        = 'X'

      i_tab_raw_data       = lt_raw_data

      i_filename           = p_file

    TABLES

      i_tab_converted_data = <ft_data>

    EXCEPTIONS

      conversion_failed    = 1

      OTHERS               = 2.

  IF sy-subrc <> 0.

    MESSAGE ID sy-msgid TYPE sy-msgty NUMBER sy-msgno

    WITH sy-msgv1 sy-msgv2 sy-msgv3 sy-msgv4.

  ENDIF.

 

ENDFORM.

*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*

*& Form upload_table

*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*

*& text

*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*

*& -->  p1        text

*& <--  p2        text

*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*

FORM upload_table.

 

  "Local Variables

  FIELD-SYMBOLS: <fs_value> TYPE any.

 

  DATA: lv_fieldname         TYPE dfies-lfieldname,

        lt_value_list        TYPE TABLE OF soli,

        ls_message           TYPE syst,

        lv_value_internal    TYPE c,

        lt_failure_tab       TYPE TABLE OF ddfkeyrc,

        ls_failure_tab       TYPE ddfkeyrc,

        lt_additional_fields TYPE dcfielddats,

        ls_additional_fields TYPE LINE OF dcfielddats.

  DATA: lv_row_number TYPE i,

        lv_col_number TYPE i.

 

  "Get Table Info

  CALL FUNCTION 'DDIF_TABL_GET'

    EXPORTING

      name          = p_table

      state         = 'A'

      langu         = sy-langu

    TABLES

      dd03p_tab     = gt_dd03p

    EXCEPTIONS

      illegal_input = 1

      OTHERS        = 2.

  IF sy-subrc <> 0.

  ENDIF.

 

  SORT gt_dd03p BY position.

 

  "Validate each Row

  REFRESH: gt_error_report[].

 

  LOOP AT <ft_data> ASSIGNING <fs_data>.

 

    "Row Number

    lv_row_number = sy-tabix.

 

    "Validate Each Field

    LOOP AT gt_dd03p ASSIGNING <fs_dd03p>.

 

      "Column Number

      lv_col_number = sy-tabix.

 

      lv_fieldname = <fs_dd03p>-fieldname.

 

      "Get the Value

      ASSIGN COMPONENT <fs_dd03p>-fieldname OF STRUCTURE <fs_data>

                    TO <fs_value>.

      IF sy-subrc NE 0.

        CONTINUE.

      ENDIF.

 

      "Validate the Field/Value

      REFRESH: lt_failure_tab[].

 

 

      "Collect the errors

      LOOP AT lt_failure_tab INTO ls_failure_tab.

        CLEAR gs_error_report.

        gs_error_report-type = 'E'.

        gs_error_report-id = ls_failure_tab-arbgb.

        gs_error_report-number = ls_failure_tab-msgnr.

        MESSAGE ID ls_failure_tab-arbgb TYPE 'I' NUMBER ls_failure_tab-msgnr

        WITH ls_failure_tab-msgv1 ls_failure_tab-msgv2

             ls_failure_tab-msgv3 ls_failure_tab-msgv4

        INTO gs_error_report-message.

        gs_error_report-message_v1  = ls_failure_tab-msgv1.

        gs_error_report-message_v2 = ls_failure_tab-msgv2.

        gs_error_report-message_v3 = ls_failure_tab-msgv3.

        gs_error_report-message_v4 = ls_failure_tab-msgv3.

        gs_error_report-parameter = ls_failure_tab-keyvalue.

        gs_error_report-row = lv_row_number.

        gs_error_report-field  = ls_failure_tab-fieldname.

        gs_error_report-system  = lv_col_number.

        APPEND gs_error_report TO gt_error_report.

      ENDLOOP.

 

    ENDLOOP.

 

  ENDLOOP.

 

  "Check if there are any errors

  IF gt_error_report[] IS NOT INITIAL.

    RETURN.

  ENDIF.

 

  "Local Task for Update

  SET UPDATE TASK LOCAL.

 

  "Modify

  IF p_refr EQ 'X'.

    DELETE FROM (p_table).

  ENDIF.

 

  MODIFY (p_table) FROM TABLE <ft_data>.

  IF sy-subrc EQ 0.

    COMMIT WORK AND WAIT.

  ELSE.

    ROLLBACK WORK.

  ENDIF.

 

ENDFORM.

*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*

*& Form display_report

*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*

*& text

*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*

*& -->  p1        text

*& <--  p2        text

*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*

FORM display_report.

 

  "Local Variables

  TYPE-POOLS: slis.

  DATA: lv_alv_callback_program TYPE sy-repid,

        ls_alv_layout           TYPE slis_layout_alv.

 

  lv_alv_callback_program = sy-repid.

 

  ls_alv_layout-zebra = 'X'.

  ls_alv_layout-colwidth_optimize = 'X'.

 

  "Display Report

  IF gt_error_report[] IS INITIAL.

    CALL FUNCTION 'REUSE_ALV_GRID_DISPLAY'

      EXPORTING

        i_interface_check           = ' '

        i_bypassing_buffer          = ' '

        i_buffer_active             = ' '

        i_callback_program          = lv_alv_callback_program

        i_callback_pf_status_set    = ' '

        i_callback_user_command     = ' '

        i_callback_top_of_page      = ' '

        i_callback_html_top_of_page = ' '

        i_callback_html_end_of_list = ' '

        i_structure_name            = p_table

        i_background_id             = ' '

        is_layout                   = ls_alv_layout

      TABLES

        t_outtab                    = <ft_data>

      EXCEPTIONS

        program_error               = 1

        OTHERS                      = 2.

    IF sy-subrc <> 0.

      MESSAGE ID sy-msgid TYPE sy-msgty NUMBER sy-msgno

      WITH sy-msgv1 sy-msgv2 sy-msgv3 sy-msgv4.

    ENDIF.

  ELSE.

    CALL FUNCTION 'REUSE_ALV_GRID_DISPLAY'

      EXPORTING

        i_interface_check           = ' '

        i_bypassing_buffer          = ' '

        i_buffer_active             = ' '

        i_callback_program          = lv_alv_callback_program

        i_callback_pf_status_set    = ' '

        i_callback_user_command     = ' '

        i_callback_top_of_page      = ' '

        i_callback_html_top_of_page = ' '

        i_callback_html_end_of_list = ' '

        i_structure_name            = 'BAPIRET2'

        i_background_id             = ' '

        is_layout                   = ls_alv_layout

      TABLES

        t_outtab                    = gt_error_report

      EXCEPTIONS

        program_error               = 1

        OTHERS                      = 2.

    IF sy-subrc <> 0.

      MESSAGE ID sy-msgid TYPE sy-msgty NUMBER sy-msgno

      WITH sy-msgv1 sy-msgv2 sy-msgv3 sy-msgv4.

    ENDIF.

  ENDIF.

 

ENDFORM.

*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*

*& Form initialization

*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*

*& text

*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*

*& -->  p1        text

*& <--  p2        text

*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*

FORM initialization .

 

  "Create dynamic internal table

  CREATE DATA gt_data TYPE TABLE OF (p_table).

  ASSIGN gt_data->* TO <ft_data>.

 

  "Create dynamic work area

  CREATE DATA gs_data TYPE (p_table).

  ASSIGN gs_data->* TO <fs_data>.

 

ENDFORM.

Selection Screen :

 

 

Key Concepts:

  1. Dynamic Table Creation Based on Structure Name:
    • The lv_structure_name is used to specify the table's structure dynamically.
    • CREATE DATA lt_dynamic TYPE TABLE OF (lv_structure_name) dynamically creates a table based on the structure name passed.
  2. Appending Data:
    • In the example, the code simulates inserting data into the dynamic table based on the structure SFLIGHT. You would change this logic depending on the structure of the dynamic table you're working with.
  3. Field Symbols:
    • The field symbols (<fs_dynamic>, <fs_row>) allow you to reference and modify the data dynamically. You modify rows based on conditions, like carrid or connid.
  4. Modifying Data:
    • The MODIFY statement is used to update the rows of the dynamic internal table.

Conclusion:

Dynamic table updates in SAP ABAP allow you to work with tables whose structures are not known at compile-time. This provides flexibility when dealing with data from various sources or working with user-defined structures. The key challenge is ensuring that the correct structure is used and modifying data efficiently at runtime.

entry.title: Document 4500006700 does not contain any selectable items while doing transfer posting 411 K
entry.content:
In consignment process while doing Transfer posting with reference to PO got following issue,
and i released po, and successfully post the gr with movement type 101 for the same 

Document 4500006700   does not contain any selectable items

Message no. M7064

Diagnosis

The document or document item entered cannot be adopted.

Possible reasons for this are:

  • The document items have already been processed
    (Suggest zero lines indicator set?).
  • the document items have been deleted or are blocked.
  • the document items have been created for a different plant.
  • (for reservations only) movements are not yet allowed for the reservation items.
  • (for reservations only) the document items are retrograde components of the order. These are posted using the order confirmations.
  • A confirmation must exist for the document items.
  • The document items must be released first.
  • The document is an SAP document.

 

Procedure

Make sure your entries are correct.

  • If the system has already processed the total quantity, you can set the Suggest zero lines indicator. The system will suggest the quantity zero for the items that have already been processed.
  • If you have entered a particular item, cancel the item. If you do not enter an item, the system searches all of the open items in the document.
  • If the document is a purchase order, production order or a reservation, you can enter search criteria in the dialog box.
  • If the document is an SAP document, you have to cancel it manually by entering the reversal movement type.

entry.title: Word templates using CAP
entry.content:
Use case

In a recent BTP application, I needed to generate Word documents by combining dynamic data and images with a predefined template. This requirement came from the need to automate the creation of structured, professional documents—such as reports or invoices—directly from the application, saving time and reducing manual effort.

Solution

To address this, I built a CAP application that manages both the data and the document templates. The application allows users to store and maintain multiple templates, which can include placeholders for dynamic content. When a document is requested, the system retrieves the relevant data, merges it with the chosen template, and generates a Word document for download. This approach provides flexibility in handling various document formats—such as reports or invoices—while ensuring the content is dynamically updated based on the stored data.

Technical details

Create a CAP project and install the following npm package:

https://www.npmjs.com/package/easy-template-x

 

In the schema.cds in the db folder, add the following entities:

  • Entity Books that will be used to store the data
  • Entity WordTemplate to store the template documents

In the service, expose the books entity and wordtemplate entity. In addition, create a new entity that will be used as a virtual entity to get the documents filled with the database data:

 

In the service handler, create an eventhandler for the virtual entity on the READ event:

 

In this eventhandler, first load the data from the book table:

 

            const book = await tx.run(SELECT.one.from(Books).where({ ID: bookId }));
            if (!book) {
                return req.reject(404, 'No book found for ID:' + bookId);
            }

Get the template:

 const templates = await tx.run(SELECT.columns('templateId','content').from(WordTemplate).where({ templateId: templateId }));
            if (templates.length === 0) {
                return req.reject(404, 'No template found for templateid:' + templateId);
            }

 

The query returns a stream which needs to be converted to a buffer:

const templateContent = await stream2buffer(templates[0].content);

 

Proivde an object with some static data, an image and the book data from the database. Create a wordtemplate instance and use it to process the data object:

let wordData = {prop1:"hello world!",
        image1: {
            _type: "image",
            source: fs.readFileSync(path.resolve(__dirname, "./img/hero.jpg")),
            format: "image/jpeg",
            width: 200,
            height: 200
        },...book
};          
            const handler = new wordTemplate.TemplateHandler();
            const doc = await handler.process(templateContent, wordData);

 

The result of the parsing process needs to be returned with the mimetype and filename:

 return {
                value: Readable.from(doc),
                $mediaContentType: 'application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.wordprocessingml.document',
                $mediaContentDispositionFilename: 'test.docx'
            };

Result

First create an entry for the wordtemplate:

 

Create a template document:

Upload the template:

Test the result by opening the browser:

http://localhost:4004/odata/v4/catalog/WordAttachments(templateId=1,bookId=1)/content

 

 

Project

You can find the full demo project on GitHub:

https://github.com/lemaiwo/cap-word-template

The project contains a test.http file with all the needed requests to test this:

https://github.com/lemaiwo/cap-word-template/blob/main/tests/test.http

With a template: https://github.com/lemaiwo/cap-word-template/blob/main/tests/template.docx

Invoice Processing with Document Management Service

They have given sample automation for downloading the file from DMS, you can modify it as per your requirement and use it.

or 

There are also DMS activities available in automation that you can use.

https://help.sap.com/doc/77f2293985d7420aabecc0c8d0647e46/Cloud/en-US/modules/dms.html#select

You can also check following blog for more info. -- A Comprehensive Guide to Downloading Files in SAP Build Process Automation and Utilizing BTP DMS in SAP Build Process Automation automations and forms 

Before all this you need to create DMS destination in BTP subaccount, you can refer following link -- DMS destination

After downloading the file from the DMS to your server, you can attach it to an Outlook email using the 'Send Email' activity. Alternatively, you can use Python to send the email via SMTP.

entry.title: Re: SUB: Create Sales Order in S/4HANA from Excel via Bui...
entry.content:
Hi Mohith,

A diagram like the below:-

 

For the controller part:-

onUpload: function (oEvent) {
    const that = this;
    const file = oEvent.getParameter("files")[0];

    if (!file) {
        MessageToast.show("Please choose a file.");
        return;
    }

    const reader = new FileReader();
    reader.onload = function (e) {
        const data = e.target.result;

        // Parse the Excel file
        const workbook = XLSX.read(data, { type: "binary" });

        // Modify contents of the first sheet
        const firstSheetName = workbook.SheetNames[0];
        const worksheet = workbook.Sheets[firstSheetName];
        const json = XLSX.utils.sheet_to_json(worksheet, { defval: "" });

        // Example: Add a new column to each row
        const modifiedData = json.map((row, index) => {
            row["New Column"] = "Row " + (index + 1);
            return row;
        });

        // Convert JSON back to worksheet
        const newWorksheet = XLSX.utils.json_to_sheet(modifiedData);

        // Replace the original sheet
        workbook.Sheets[firstSheetName] = newWorksheet;

        // Generate new Excel file
        const wbout = XLSX.write(workbook, { bookType: 'xlsx', type: 'binary' });

        // Convert to Blob and trigger download
        const blob = new Blob([s2ab(wbout)], { type: "application/octet-stream" });
        saveAs(blob, "Modified_Excel.xlsx"); // Use FileSaver.js or your own downloader

        MessageToast.show("Excel processed and downloaded!");
    };

    reader.readAsBinaryString(file);
},

// Utility function to convert string to ArrayBuffer
function s2ab(s) {
    const buf = new ArrayBuffer(s.length);
    const view = new Uint8Array(buf);
    for (let i = 0; i < s.length; i++) view[i] = s.charCodeAt(i) & 0xff;
    return buf;
}

Please ensure 'xlsx.full.min.js' from SheetJS(link)

Thanks,

Jakes
